Savukārt mīlestību pret mūziku pārbauda "Melomaniakos". Autosports ir liela daļa Mārtiņa dzīves - ne tikai skatīties un filmēt, bet arī pašam kādreiz izbraukt.

programme.title: Sporta studija
programme.desc: "Mūsu uzņemtais virziens izsakāms neformālā sauklī "sports sabiedrībai!". Šis virziens tika uzsākts jau iepriekš ar "Tautas sporta" sižetu sērijām un sporta laukumu kampaņām. Turpmāk LTV Tautas sporta sižeti būs tradicionāla "Sporta studijas" sastāvdaļa. "Sporta Studiju" vadīs rūdītie sporta žurnālisti Dāvids Ernštreits un Reinis Ošenieks, kā arī jaunās sporta žurnālistikas zvaigznes Andris Auziņš, Matīss Timofejevs un Raimonds Gekišs. "Sporta studijas" aktualitātēm sekojiet https://www.facebook.com/LTVSportaStudija

programme.title: Province
programme.desc: Savā darba ikdienā Inga Saliņa ir valsts iestādes darbiniece. Reiz ļaunais murgs - pandēmijas laiks - ar savu mājsēdi Ingai rezultējās kā visai ražīgs. Kamēr citi tolaik nezināja, ko iesākt, nīka garlaicībā, grima apātijā vai krita atkarībās, viņa savā senču vecsaimniecībā Madonas pusē izlēma ierīkot kārtīgu ofisu attālinātajam darbam un ķērās klāt senajai kūtij. Pirmais savām rokām tapa jauks darba ofiss telekonferencēm un "zūmiem" - nevienam nav jāzina, ka tur reiz mitinājās cūkas un vistas. Un maisam gals bija vaļā. Inga pati trimmerē bērzu jaunaudzi, pieskata bišu saimi un pie katras izdevības kurina pirtskrāsni, lai palutinātu radus un draugus ar kārtīgu lauku pirts garu Bet ir brīži, kad nosvērtā un mierīgā Inga pārvēršas par pavisam citu cilvēku. Un tas ir HOKEJS. Viņa pati smej, ka ar šo atkarību jau esot aplipinājusi arī savu māsu bērnus. Un goda lieta Ingai ir jau vairāk nekā divus gadu desmitus doties uz pasaules čempionātiem, lai kur arī tie notiktu, lai kā kaislīgs fans līdzdzīvotu Latvijas izlases spēlēm.

programme.title: Ielas garumā
programme.desc: Kantora iela pie Mārupītes Rīgas pilsētas plānā ir no 1902. gada, un turpat 100 gadus tā bija samērā klusa, gleznaina iela ar mazstāvu apbūvi Bieriņos. Tagad iela savieno Bieriņus ar Mārupi un kļuvusi par automaģistrāli. Šajā raidījumā izstaigāsim ielas senākos kvartālus, kuru vēsture cieši saistīta ar Bieriņmuižu un baronu fon Rautenfeldu dzimtas sarežģīto likteņstāstu. Sava vieta ielas krāsainajā audumā ir pulkvežleitnantam Ādolfam Kontrovskim, kuru padomju vara nošāva Soļikamskā. Kolorīta personība bijis tirgotājs Žanis Ķezbers, kurš esot Garozas prototips Viļa Lāča romānā "Zvejnieka dēls", bet dzīvē - pavisam savādāks. Tepat pie Mārupītes tapuši daudzi Leo Kokles darbi, bet izcili inženieri un automehāniķi ir ielas firmas zīme joprojām.

programme.title: Komisārs Reksis
programme.desc: Katja un Lorenco dodas brīvdienu braucienā ar kruīza kuģi, tomēr Reksis nav ar mieru, ka tiek atstāts krastā, un attiecīgi rīkojas. Uz klāja Katja iepazīstas ar meiteni Annu, kura ir cietusi no vardarbības. Katja viņu uzaicina vakariņās, bet Anna neierodas, un viss liecina, ka viņa ir pazudusi bez pēdām. Meklēšanas operācija atklāj baisu noziedzības tīklu, kurā meitene bijusi iesaistīta.

programme.title: Komisārs Reksis
programme.desc: Lorenco, Morīni un Reksis ir ieradušies uz policijas konferenci saulainajā Maltā. Tajā pašā dienā Valletas bibliotēkā lasītāju acu priekšā tiek noindēts mākslas profesors. Atklājas, ka viņš ne tikai tikko pabeidzis novērtējumu nezināmai un ļoti dārgai Karavadžo gleznai, bet arī nopircis biļetes uz tālo Tunisiju un Abū Dabī. Reksis ar kolēģiem dodas noziegumam pa pēdām mākslas pasaulē.

programme.title: Komisārs Reksis
programme.desc: Vīrietis, spēlējoties ar savu suni, nejauši atrod kāda cilvēka mirstīgās atliekas. Izrādās, ka mirušais ir liela farmācijas uzņēmuma vadītāja dēls, kurš pirms vairākiem gadiem pazudis bez vēsts. Ierodoties komisariātā, Lorenco un Reksi sagaida pārsteigums – ģimenes apstākļu dēļ Morīni ir pārcēlies uz Milānu un viņa vietā darbā tiek norīkots inspektors Alberto Monterosso.

programme.title: Komisārs Reksis
programme.desc: Kādā Romas zivju veikalā tiek nogalināts puisis, kurš piepelnījies, tirgojot narkotikas. Šī slepkavība noved pie nežēlīgu notikumu ķēdes, tajos ir iesaistīta nolaupīta meitene, noindēts sargsuns, narkobarons un kontrabandisti no Kolumbijas. Reksim tiek uzticēta galvenā loma sarežģītā ilgi meklēta noziedznieka noķeršanas operācijā. Kad operācija ir pabeigta, Lorenco nejauši pieļauj traģisku kļūdu, par kuru samaksā visaugstāko cenu.

programme.title: Komisārs Reksis
programme.desc: Savā guļamistabā tiek atrasta nosista ietekmīga advokāta sieva. Par notikušo vainu nekavējoties uzņemas pats advokāts, tomēr izmeklēšanas gaitā viņa atzīšanās raisa pārāk daudzus jautājumus. Komisariātā Lorenco amatā darbā tiek norīkots komisārs Davide Rivera, kurš lietišķi un efektīvi ķeras pie lietas izmeklēšanas. Tomēr iespējams, ka viņa sarežģītākais uzdevums būs iegūt sērojošā Rekša draudzību un uzticību.

programme.title: Zinātnes noslēpumi
programme.desc: Dažādas mūsu ikdienā ierastas darbības un notikumi, izskaidroti no zinātnes viedokļa.

programme.title: Futbols. Baltijas kauss
programme.desc: Klāt Baltijas kausa izcīņa, faktiski senākais futbola turnīrs Eiropā valstsvienību līmenī, un Latvijai pirmā spēle ir pusfināls pret Lietuvu. Šoreiz šī spēle mūsu izlasei jāaizvada izbraukumā Kauņā. Maija vidū Latvijas Futbola federācija pagarināja līgumu ar Itālijas treneri Paolo Nikolato, un tas būs spēkā līdz 2027. gada novembrim. Latvijai izdevās saglabāt vietu Nāciju līgas trešajā līgā, C divīzijā, un nu būs jāspēlē arī Eiropas čempionāta kvalifikācijā. Jācer uz veiksmīgu iznākumu arī Baltijas līgā, otra spēle Latvijai paredzēta jaunnedēļ, 9. jūnijā, Rīgā, turklāt pirmo reizi izlase spēlēs LNK Sporta parkā. Pretiniekos būs Igaunija vai Fēru salas, atkarībā no rezultāta pusfinālos. Redzēsim, vai komandai varēs pievienoties pilnīgi visi mūsu labākie futbolisti, bet tā ir iespējami laba pārbaude arī talantīgajiem jaunajiem spēlētājiem, kas sevi apliecinājuši virslīgā.

programme.title: Policijas iecirknis
programme.desc: Kā jau ierasts, lietu ir tik daudz, ka daži komandas biedri iecirknī strādā arī sestdienā. Floransa pieprasa Sallijai novērošanas komandu pazudušo bezpajumtnieku lietā. Daniels risina asas sarunas ar Patriku, Bruno un Stefanu saistībā ar Lorānu Klutjē, kurš tiek turēts aizdomās par Gladū nāvi.

programme.title: Policijas iecirknis
programme.desc: Patriks izjautā Lorānu par Gladū. Pazudušo bezpajumtnieku lietā aizdomās turamais Džans Mins atbrīvots un tiek novērots. Bruno dodas uz tiesu tikties ar kriminālizmeklētāju saistībā ar Žiljetu Riopelu. Noelija atklāj, ka pie Patrika ilgāku laiku viesojusies Stefānija.

programme.title: Policijas iecirknis
programme.desc: Žandarmērijas krimināllietu izmeklēšana grib pārņemt izmeklēšanu par Pjēra-Īva Gladū nāvi. Komanda saņem Gladū autopsijas ziņojumu ar DNS rezultātiem. Nansija turpina saņemt anonīmu informāciju un norādes saistībā ar 31. iecirkņa nomelnošanu.

programme.title: Policijas iecirknis
programme.desc: Atbrīvotais Džans Mins pazūd. Noelija saņem zvanu no vīrieša, kuram nošauts suns, un vīrietis dedzīgi apsūdz savu kaimiņu. Nansijas advokāts mēģina viņu atrunāt no pārsteidzīgiem soļiem un nemeklēt sazvērestības. Tomēr Nansija zvana Briēram, lai viņš uzraksta rakstu.

programme.title: Policijas iecirknis
programme.desc: Floransa un Noelija izmeklē suņa slepkavības lietu. Pagaidām aizdomās turamais ir kaimiņš, ar kuru suņa īpašniekam ir naidīgas attiecības; izskatās, ka iepītas arī mīlas lietas. Stefans cenšas iegūt Bokasīni medicīnisko izrakstu un atklāj, ka dokumenti ir safabricēti.

programme.title: Neej uz randiņu ar Brendonu!
programme.desc: Stāsts balstīts uz patiesiem notikumiem par Emberu, kura, izmantojot iepazīšanās lietotnes, satiek šķietami ideālu vīrieti – Brendonu Džonsonu. Viņu straujās romantiskās attiecības drīz vien pārtop laulībā, taču tad Embera sāk pamanīt dīvainas un satraucošas lietas. Kopā ar Brendona bijušo sievu Atīnu viņa atklāj virkni melu, manipulāciju un bīstamu uzvedību. Atklājas, ka Brendonam ir pagātne – vairākas sievietes un pat tiesas spriedumi. Šo sieviešu stāsts kalpo kā brīdinājums par toksiskām attiecībām, krāpšanu un vardarbību.

programme.title: Midsomeras slepkavības
programme.desc: Detektīvs Toms Bārnabijs ir pacietīgs un metodisks izmeklētājs. Viņš saprot, ka noziegumiem mazā pilsētiņā vienmēr būs sociāls raksturs, tamdēļ svarīgi izprast tās iedzīvotāju attiecības. Tikai un vienīgi viņš spēj pamanīt neskaidrus mājienus un sadzīt pēdas viltīgam slepkavam brīdī, kad nupat, šķiet, tas izbēgs nesodīts. Kad Toms Bārnabijs atgriežas mājās, viņš ir mīlošs vīrs un tēvs, tomēr, neskatoties uz to, detektīvs viņā nekad nesnauž. Citreiz vistrāpīgākās atklāsmes rodas tieši pie brokastu galda! Seriāls "Midsomeras slepkavības" ieturēts vislabākajās britu klasiskā detektīva tradīcijās: virkne aizdomās turamo, sajauktas pēdas, mīklaini mājieni, neizprotami motīvi un iespēja skatītājam pašam minēt, kurš šoreiz būs vainīgais. Seriāls veiksmīgi izrādīts teju visās Eiropas valstīs, kā arī ASV, Kanādā, Dienvidāfrikā un Tuvajos Austrumos, kopumā tas tiek uzņemts jau vairāk nekā 25 gadus.

programme.title: Noziegumi Amalfi piekrastē
programme.desc: Amalfi, Itālija. Starp saulē mirdzošām klintīm un tirkīza viļņiem Daniels De Luka, bijušais jūras kājnieks ar noslēpumainu pagātni, strādā greznajā viesnīcā "Kostjēra", risinot viesu problēmas un untumus. Kad kādu nakti viesnīcas īpašnieka jaunākā meita Aliče pazūd bez vēsts, Daniels un viņa uzticamie pavadoņi seko pavedieniem, kas atklāj tumšu noslēpumu tīklu aiz glancētajām viesnīcas fasādēm un bagāto klientu kaprīzēm. Katrs pavediens ved viņus dziļāk intrigās un atklāj, ka pat visskaistākajā piekrastē nekas nav tā, kā tas izskatās.

programme.title: Melnais saraksts
programme.desc: Kritiķu un skatītāju iemīļots kriminālseriāls. Tā darbība sākas dienā, kad darbu FIB galvenajā birojā Vašingtonā uzsāk jauna profilētāja Elizabete Kīna. Tajā pašā rītā tur ierodas viens no FIB meklētākajiem noziedzniekiem, Reimonds "Reds" Redingtons, un labprātīgi ļauj sevi notvert. Reds palīdz FIB sagūstīt citu bīstamu noziedznieku un novērst uzbrukumu civiliedzīvotājiem. Viņš paziņo, ka viņam esot vesels saraksts un informācija par tādiem noziedzniekiem un teroristiem, par kuru esamību FIB pat nenojauš. Reds ir gatavs sadarboties ar vienu savādu noteikumu – viņš runās tikai ar Elizabeti. Tā sākas īpašas attiecības, kuru īstā daba un iemesls ir zināmi tikai Redingtonam. Lai gan pamatā seriāla darbība norit ASV, tas aizved arī uz dažādām eksotiskām vietām visā pasaulē un ļauj ieskatīties kriminālajā pasaulē. Dažādiem apbalvojumiem vairāku gadu laikā izvirzīti seriāla specefektu veidotāji, kaskadieri un muzikālais noformējums.

programme.title: Globalizācijas un mēs
programme.desc: Deutsche Welle žurnāls.

programme.title: Literatūre
programme.desc: "Literatūre" ir literārā tūrisma dokumentāls raidījumu cikls, kas apskata latviešu literatūru no sendienām līdz mūsdienām. Katrā epizodē kāds latviešu rakstnieks kā ceļa karti ņem kādu sev tuva aizgājušo laiku autora grāmatu un kopā ar literatūristi Martu dodas ceļojumā uz vietām, kur tas dzīvojis vai par kurām rakstījis. Savukārt, viņu savrupais ceļabiedrs Gustavs ceļo atsevišķi ar grāmatām un meklē, kam palasīt priekšā.

programme.title: 1:1. Aktuālā intervija
programme.desc: Raidījumā "1:1" [viens pret vienu] satiekas sabiedrībā zināma personība un žurnālists. Raidījums skaidro, analizē un ļauj izprast sabiedrībai nozīmīgas norises visdažādākajās dzīves jomās Latvijā un pasaulē, izprast personības nozīmīgumu, atbildību šo procesu virzībā. Viesi izvēlēti, ņemot vērā tā brīža aktualitātes vai arī konkrētās personas iespējamo ietekmi uz notikumiem nākotnē, – valsts pirmās amatpersonas, biznesa, kultūras un nevalstiskā sektora pārstāvji. Raidījumu vada žurnālists Gundars Rēders.

programme.title: Vides fakti
programme.desc: Raidījums par norisēm dabā un apkārtējā vidē. "Vides fakti" sniedz informāciju par sezonālām norisēm dabā un dzīvnieku pasaulē, par vides aktualitātēm, par videi draudzīgu dzīvesveidu, par aktuālām cilvēka un vides attiecībām ārpus Latvijas robežām. Raidījumā "Vides fakti" ir skatāmi arī piedzīvojumu sižeti par aktīvā tūrisma iespējām Latvijā, kā arī īsfilmas par neparastiem ceļojumiem pasaulē.

programme.title: Kultūrdeva
programme.desc: Izklaidējošs un informatīvs kultūras sarunu šovs par kultūras notikumiem un procesiem Latvijā.

programme.title: Lielās patiesības
programme.desc: Vai kari ir cilvēces attīstības neatņemama cikliska parādība? Vai kari, neskatoties uz to šausmām un postu, cilvēcei ir devuši arī kādu "restartu" - ekonomisku, zinātnisku, morālu? "Karošanas instinkts" ir cilvēka zemapziņā, ko viegli iedarbina prasmīga propaganda. Paradoksālie minējumi, ka karā lielākie zaudētāji un cietēji ilglaicīgākā perspektīvā ir uzvarētāji. Kara Ukrainā iespējamās morālās sekas Eiropā, Ukrainā un Krievijā. Par šiem jautājumiem raidījuma vadītājs Ilmārs Latkovskis sarunājas ar vēsturnieku Valdi Kuzminu.

programme.title: Daudz laimes, jubilār!
programme.desc: Dramatiskais tenors Aleksandrs Antoņenko, starptautiska mēroga operas zvaigzne, viens no mūsu slavenākajiem māksliniekiem, jūnijā ir Latvijā ar vairākiem koncertiem, izbrīvējot laiku arī sarunai raidījumā "Daudz laimes, jubilār!". Savulaik Valsts filharmonijas un operas, vēlāk Vācijas operteātru solists, saulaina baritona īpašnieks Aleksandrs Poļakovs izteicies: "Skaistas balsis ir daudziem, bet, lai stāvētu viens daudzgalvainas publikas priekšā, uz lielās operas skatuves, tur vajag iekšēju spēku un jaudu - to katrs nevar! Vienkārši - netur nervi!" Nav šaubu, ka Aleksandram Antoņenko ir ne tikai izcils vokāls, bet varbūt vēl lielāks gars, - ne velti viņu aicinājuši izcili operteātri Ņujorkā, Vīnē, Londonā, Romā, Milānā, Diseldorfā, Berlīnē, Frankfurtē, Minhenē, Grācā un citur pasaulē. Dziedātāja repertuārā ir vadošās tenora partijas: titulloma Dž. Verdi operā "Otello", Kavaradosi (Dž. Pučīni "Toska"), Turidu (P. Maskanji "Zemnieka gods"), Kanio (R. Leonkavallo "Pajaci"), De Grijē (Dž. Pučīni "Manona Lesko"), Kalafs (Dž. Pučīni "Turandota"), Gabriels Adorno (Dž. Verdi "Simons Bokanegra"), Radamess (Dž. Verdi "Aīda"), Diks Džonsons (Dž. Pučīni "Meitene no Rietumiem"), Dons Hosē (Ž. Bizē "Karmena"), Samsons (K. Sensānsa "Samsons un Dalila"), Princis (A. Dvoržāka "Nāra"), Hermanis (P. Čaikovska "Pīķa dāma"), Ismails (Dž. Verdi "Nabuko") u. c.. Saņēmis dažādas profesionālās balvas, tai skaitā Jusi Bjerlinga balvu, Austrijas "Grand Prix de la Culture", vairākkārt "Lielo mūzikas balvu", Latvijas Izcilības balvu kultūrā, kā arī Triju Zvaigžņu ordeni. Būdams Latvijā, Aleksandrs allaž tiekas un rūpējas par savu - un daudzu citu izcilu mākslinieku - skolotāju un vokālo pedagoģi Margaritu Gruzdevu, ko savulaik aicinājis arī uz savām pasaules pirmizrādēm ārzemju operteātros. Raidījumā paralēli profesionāliem stāstiem Aleksandrs dalās ar operdzīves aizkulisēm, niansēm, kur dažādiem sadarbības partneriem, tai skaitā pedagogiem, kā arī veiksmei ir liela nozīme. Jāatzīst, ka būtiska nozīme ir dziedātāja harismai, temperamentam, jūtu amplitūdai, fiziskajai sagatavotībai un pat humora izjūtai. Antoņenko pieskandina telpu ne tikai ar savu vokālo sniegumu, bet arī ar savu versmaino, jokus un provokācijas mīlošo, izaicinājumus pieņemošo prātu, tāpēc saruna raidījumā raisās dzīva un aizraujoša.

programme.title: V.I.P. - Veiksme. Intuīcija. Prāts
programme.desc: Starptautiskā bērnu aizsardzības diena ir 1. jūnijs. Bērnu gan pie Uģa Joksta nebūs, būs viņu vecāki no organizācijām, par kurām var teikt - tās ir mūžam jaunas, tradīcijām bagātas, apvieno visas paaudzes. Tās ir akadēmiskās mūžoganizācijas. Kapteiņi: Jūlija Trofimova un Roberts Šilings. Jūlija Trofimova - komandas kapteine, akadēmiskās mūžorganizācijas "Sororitas Tatiana" seniore. Angļu valodas filoloģe, interesējas par vēsturi un mākslu, kas ir pašsaprotami, jo strādā muzejā "Rīgas Jūgendstila centrs" par projektu koordinatori. Sabiedriski aktīva un komunikabla, "Sororitas Tatiana" korporācijā iesaistījusi arī savu meitu. Viktorija Oļševska - "Sororitas Tatiana" biedre. Studē Latvijas Universitātē, strādā par matemātikas skolotāju vidusskolā, ir radošs cilvēks ar daudzām un dažādām interesēm. Viņa var būt tetovētāja, tulkotāja un intelektuālo spēļu vadītāja, bet visvairāk patīk darbs skolā un sadarbība ar skolēniem. Marija Paulāne - "Sororitas Tatiana" komiltone, RTU Būvniecības un mašīnzinību fakultātes 4. kursa studente. Meitene no Rēzeknes, kas domā, ka diennaktī ir 40 stundas, mēģina paspēt visu, par ko vien aizdomājas. Mīl dvēselīgas kompānijas un gardas bulciņas ar kafiju. Labprāt nodarbojas ar skolēniem un studentiem papildnodarbībās, pasniedzot matemātiku, fiziku un ķīmiju. Roberts Šilings - komandas kapteinis, akadēmiskās organizācijas "Konkordija Valdemārija" biedrs kopš 1990. gada, filistrs. Darbojas tūrisma nozarē. Latviskās dzīvesziņas izzinātājs un skolotājs, pazīstams arī kā Meža Roberts. Latviešu saieta "Trejdeviņi" un Latvisko rituālu vadītāju skolas dibinātājs. Patīk makšķerēt, īpaši ārzemēs. Edgars Maļinovskis - akadēmiskās organizācijas "Konkordija Valdemārija" seniors, krāsnesis. Rūpējas par laikapstākļu novērojumu kvalitāti valsts monitoringa tīklā, savukārt brīvajā laikā bieži sastopams basketbola zālē pie sekretariāta galdiņa; regulāri ar draugiem piedalās prāta spēlēs. Mārtiņš Berts - pēc izglītības ir ģeogrāfs, kurš vismaz padsmit gadus piedalās dažādās erudīcijas spēlēs; ir no slavenās Ģeogrāfu mafijas, kurā iegūti vairāki kausi "Prāta spēlēs". Savukārt mīlestību pret mūziku pārbauda "Melomaniakos". Autosports ir liela daļa Mārtiņa dzīves - ne tikai skatīties un filmēt, bet arī pašam kādreiz izbraukt.

programme.title: Pasaules panorāma
programme.desc: Globālo ziņu laikmetā notikums Āzijā vai Amerikas kontinentā jau rīt var mainīt dzīvi Eiropā, bet lēmums, kas pieņemts Rīgā – atbalsoties visā pasaulē. "Pasaules panorāma" piedāvā neatkarīgas un uzticamas ziņas, atspoguļojot un analizējot ārpolitikas aktualitātes, kā arī sarunas ar politologiem, ārpolitikas ekspertiem, pasaulē pazīstamām personībām par svarīgāko Baltijā un Ziemeļvalstīs, citviet Eiropā, kā arī visā pasaulē. Raidījumu vada LTV Ziņu dienesta ārzemju ziņu redaktori Toms Pastors un Ina Strazdiņa.

programme.title: Ancient China from Above
programme.desc: In episode two, we take to the air to reveal how modern China lives,eats,works,travels andplays on a mega scale. We will learn how this nation has transformed its cities and infrastructure in three decades while still retaining its traditions. We gain a greater understanding of how Chinas industrialization impacts the planet in negative ways, and how they are seeking solutions. And we find out what a billion plus population does for fun and how it crosses the countrys huge expanses.

programme.title: Air Crash Investigation
programme.desc: March 10th, 1989 Bound for Winnipeg, Air Ontario Flight #1363 prepares for takeoff after a brief layover at a remote Northern airport. Already an hour behind schedule, the pilots fear the heavy snowfall will further delay their departure, and after refueling, they taxi into position and accelerate. The jet reaches takeoff speed but struggles to get airborne, and although it clears the trees at the end of the runway, starts shaking violently and less than a minute later crashes into the woods. It takes months for investigators to determine what went wrong. And tragically, their findings come too late to prevent an icy killer from striking again.

programme.title: Air Crash Investigation
programme.desc: Every time a plane crashes, the world takes notice. And so do the experts whose job it is to figure out what happened. Air Crash Investigation uncovers the truth behind the most legendary aviation disasters, and every episode features eyewitness accounts, captivating reenactments, state-of-the-art CGI, and interviews with the investigators who ultimately determined what went wrong. This season on Air Crash Investigation: Conflicting stories emerge after a Swedish cargo plane spirals to the ground; an African airliner crashes in the wilderness and the cause proves too sinister to be believed; and, when a Pakistani passenger jet disappears on approach to Kathmandu, Nepalese investigators must determine how it ended up on a crash course with the Himalayas.

programme.title: Northwoods Survival
programme.desc: As temperatures plunge, Margot is on the hunt for some "heat" - the final step in making her Northwoods home habitable. Hoping to bring a little luxury to his backwoods abode - Billy gives an old outbuilding new life. Emilie and Gilbert have their mettle and their relationship tested as they race to finish their cabin. And Matty takes the notorious Newfoundland winter head on as he tries to complete an ambitious new "Hillbilly" building project.

programme.title: Northwoods Survival
programme.desc: As temperatures plunge, Margot looks to her dogs to help her seriously raise her wood-harvesting game. Emilie and Gilbert travel to a remote, frozen lake, hoping to net a haul of food for their family. Yearning to bring a little more light into his backwoods life, Billy begins work on an off-grid power system, and Matty heads indoors to complete the latest and perhaps greatest of all his "Hillbilly" building projects.

programme.title: Ultimate Airport Dubai
programme.desc: Dubai International, one of the fastest growing airports in the world, is working close to capacity. Delivering ambitious expansion plans while keeping it on track leads to testing times for the staff. Supervisor Mahmoud Alam has a challenge trying to help a Canadian couple bumped off a flight to their dream holiday; David Robson must greet some temperamental VIP passengers; Dispatcher Nizel has a problem when a brand new 389 million dollar A380 goes missing; and time is running out to finish the first phase of the brand new 3.2 billion dollar concourse in just 18 days.

programme.title: Ultimate Airport Dubai
programme.desc: Dubai Airport is nearing full stretch and passenger numbers are growing, so its opening a $3.2 billion dollar extension to Terminal 3 with 20 new departure gates, but not all of it is ready to launch. In Terminal 3, Passenger Operations is coping with a large group of stranded travelers. The Network Control Centre is trying to keep the Emirates fleet on schedule; and a missing passenger and his bags put their curfew-critical flight to Sydney Australia in jeopardy.

programme.title: Ancient China from Above
programme.desc: China is a land of immense scale and diversity, an ancient civilization with a history dating back thousands of years. How have the traditions of China shaped its landscape and people, and how do they hold up in the present day? To capture its scale and complexity we embark on an epic aerial journey across Chinas varied landscapes and cultures to uncover how these practices and icons endure. From the monumental engineering feats of the Great Wall to a massive water splashing festival in the tropical south, we unveil the secrets and patterns of Chinas living past in a fresh new perspective.

programme.title: Ancient China from Above
programme.desc: In episode two, we take to the air to reveal how modern China lives,eats,works,travels andplays on a mega scale. We will learn how this nation has transformed its cities and infrastructure in three decades while still retaining its traditions. We gain a greater understanding of how Chinas industrialization impacts the planet in negative ways, and how they are seeking solutions. And we find out what a billion plus population does for fun and how it crosses the countrys huge expanses.

programme.desc: Science of Stupid, series 8. Dallas Campbell hosts the show that combines serious science with some of the craziest, most spectacular user generated clips ever recorded. In each programme we show 40 to 50 clips of misadventure: ordinary folk making extraordinary mistakes and paying the price. We analyse the science behind the mishaps, with the help of bespoke animation and super slow-motion cinematography.

programme.title: Science of Stupid
programme.desc: Science of Stupid, series 8. Dallas Campbell hosts the show that combines serious science with some of the craziest, most spectacular user generated clips ever recorded. In each programme we show 40 to 50 clips of misadventure: ordinary folk making extraordinary mistakes and paying the price. We analyse the science behind the mishaps, with the help of bespoke animation and super slow-motion cinematography.

programme.title: Air Crash Investigation
programme.desc: Every time a plane crashes, the world takes notice. And so do the experts whose job it is to figure out what happened. Air Crash Investigation uncovers the truth behind the most legendary aviation disasters, and every episode features eyewitness accounts, captivating reenactments, state-of-the-art CGI, and interviews with the investigators who ultimately determined what went wrong. This season on Air Crash Investigation: Conflicting stories emerge after a Swedish cargo plane spirals to the ground; an African airliner crashes in the wilderness and the cause proves too sinister to be believed; and, when a Pakistani passenger jet disappears on approach to Kathmandu, Nepalese investigators must determine how it ended up on a crash course with the Himalayas.

programme.title: Air Crash Investigation
programme.desc: Taking off during a major storm, a brand-new 737 falls from the sky over West Africa, and an international group of investigators soon descends on Cameroon. Submerged in mud and water, Kenya Airlines Flight 507 crashed into a mangrove swamp, killing everyone on board. And while investigators ultimately pinpoint one fatal mistake that triggered the tragedy, they also uncover a lethal cascade of issues that sent the passenger jet spiralling toward the ground.

programme.title: Air Crash Investigation
programme.desc: En route from Bogota to the tiny Caribbean island of San Andres, Aires Flight 8250 is rocked by a major tropical storm. And though the crew expects to emerge from the intense weather, they are pummeled by high winds and heavy rains on their final approach and slam into the ground just short of the runway. The pilots claim the weather is to blame, but investigators ultimately reveal the cause of the accident was in their heads.

programme.title: Seconds from Disaster
programme.desc: On 26 December, 2004, the largest earthquake in 40 years strikes deep beneath the Indian Ocean. Lasting eight minutes and stretching a record 1,600 kilometres in length, the earthquake's massive strength surpasses measurement on the Richter scale. Just after 8:15 AM, billions of tonnes of water speed towards land with a force equivalent to more than 30,000 Hiroshima bombs. Within minutes, the terrifying wall of water makes landfall, engulfing entire towns in its path. Deadly currents and dangerous debris tear buildings apart, as people across South Asia fight for survival. Ultimately claiming the lives of more than 250,000 people, it is the most devastating tsunami in recorded history and scientists are puzzled by the extraordinary size and savagery of the waves. Tsunami experts must analyze the geologic episode, looking for clues behind the tsunami's overwhelming force. Their findings re-define tsunami prediction and reveal that tsunamis may pose a greater threat than previously realized.

programme.title: Superstorm New York: What Really Happened
programme.desc: Experts investigate the cause behind the storm that ravaged New York, as well as scrutinise the US government's response plans.

programme.title: The Next Mega Tsunami
programme.desc: Ten years after one of the most deadly tsunamis ever known, scientists are making a shocking discovery. Experts used to believe that the biggest killer waves were only generated in a handful of regions.

programme.title: Real Time Disaster
programme.desc: On the 10th of May 2013, a giant wave of ice ripped through homes in the small community of Ochre Beach in Manitoba, Canada. One day later, south of the boarder at Lake Millie Lacs in the United States, another strange moving wall of ice made landfall at Izatys Resort. This rare major ice shove was caught on camera. Harnessing this incredible footage we can now get closer than ever to one of natures strangest events, explore the forces at work and reveal a unique perspective on the destructive power of ice in motion.

programme.title: Real Time Disaster
programme.desc: At 9:20am on the 15th of February 2013, in the Siberian city of Chelyabinsk something, not of this world was spotted blazing its way through the morning skies. In this episode we tell the story of the 2013 Chelyabinsk Meteorite strike through the incredible footage of the event, captured by those who actually witnessed it. Well use this remarkable amateur video to unpack the science and explore where the meteorite came from, its violent end and how it unleashed a force equivalent to thirty Hiroshima bombs on the unsuspecting Russian city.

programme.title: America's Deadliest Volcano Disaster
programme.desc: Forty years ago, on May 18, 1980, Mount St. Helens erupted violently. It was the most deadly and devastating eruption in U.S. history. Combining eyewitness accounts with rarely seen images, this show reveals the unfolding apocalypse; from the first moments of the volcanic blast, to a 12-mile high ash plume and the lethal mudflows that raged down the mountain.

programme.title: Witness: Disaster in Japan
programme.desc: On March 11, 2011, a 9.0 magnitude earthquake struck 75 miles off the eastern coast of Japan. Almost immediately, tsunami warnings blared, urging residents along Japan's coast to quickly move to higher ground. For the next several hours, residents watched in stunned horror as a series of massive waves slammed into the coast, inundating entire towns and sweeping across the countryside, laying waste to everything in its path. Throughout, amateur videographers, news crews, government agencies, tourists, and countless others were recording the sights and sounds of the unfolding catastrophe. Weaving together their footage, Witness: Japan's Disaster reconstructs the earthquake and tsunami as they happened, entirely through the eyes of those who experienced them.

programme.title: Pole to Pole with Will Smith
programme.desc: Will heads to the South Pole, where temperatures can drop below minus 100 degrees Fahrenheit. He skis and treks across giant icefields, and, faced with a giant cliff of ice in bone-chilling winds, struggles to climb to the top. Only with the support of one of the world's best polar athletes, Richard Parks, does he succeed. In one of the planet's most isolated research stations, scientists take ice cores deep under the surface and make incredible sacrifices to pursue their research.

programme.title: Pole to Pole with Will Smith
programme.desc: Will, a man scared of spiders his whole life, ventures deep into the Ecuadorian Amazon on an expedition in search of deadly creatures. Joined by professor Bryan Fry and local mountaineer Carla Perez, they abseil 200 feet into a cave network known as the "womb of the Earth," where they discover a giant tarantula. Using cutting-edge techniques, they extract its venom, which could hold the key to saving millions of lives.

programme.title: Air Crash Investigation
programme.desc: Every time a plane crashes, the world takes notice. And so do the experts whose job it is to figure out what happened. Air Crash Investigation uncovers the truth behind the most legendary aviation disasters, and every episode features eyewitness accounts, captivating reenactments, state-of-the-art CGI, and interviews with the investigators who ultimately determined what went wrong. This season on Air Crash Investigation: Conflicting stories emerge after a Swedish cargo plane spirals to the ground; an African airliner crashes in the wilderness and the cause proves too sinister to be believed; and, when a Pakistani passenger jet disappears on approach to Kathmandu, Nepalese investigators must determine how it ended up on a crash course with the Himalayas.

programme.title: Air Crash Investigation
programme.desc: January 20th, 1992 High over Frances mountainous Alsace region, Air Inter Flight #148 starts its descent towards Strasbourg airport. An Airbus A320, the aircraft is the worlds first to rely mainly on computerized commands to control it, and while its autopilot had been programmed to land on a specific runway, high winds and poor winter weather mean the pilots must take over the controls to land on another runway. Slightly off course, a controller guides the pilots as they prepare to make a visual landing. But as they descend through the clouds, the aircraft suddenly rips through the treetops below and slams into the side of a mountain. Can rescuers locate the survivors in time? And can investigators figure out what caused the crash?

programme.title: Air Crash Investigation
programme.desc: Every time a plane crashes, the world takes notice. And so do the experts whose job it is to figure out what happened. Air Crash Investigation uncovers the truth behind the most legendary aviation disasters, and every episode features eyewitness accounts, captivating reenactments, state-of-the-art CGI, and interviews with the investigators who ultimately determined what went wrong. This season on Air Crash Investigation: Conflicting stories emerge after a Swedish cargo plane spirals to the ground; an African airliner crashes in the wilderness and the cause proves too sinister to be believed; and, when a Pakistani passenger jet disappears on approach to Kathmandu, Nepalese investigators must determine how it ended up on a crash course with the Himalayas.

programme.title: Black Pharaohs: Sunken Treasures
programme.desc: In a land of gold and ancient treasure, a recent discovery inside a pyramid sends archaeologists in search of clues about the glorious Nubian kingdom. But this isnt an average excavation this pyramid is flooded with water. For the first time ever, archaeologists dive inside underwater pyramid tombs to shed new light on the ancient Nubian kingdom who overthrew the pharaohs and ruled over all of Egypt for 75 years.

programme.title: Megacity Of the Maya Warrior King
programme.desc: National Geographic Explorer Albert Lin travels to the jungles of Central America to solve an ancient mystery: who defeated the most powerful Maya city to have ever existed? With the help of new technology he tracks down the mighty warrior conqueror and reveals his capital - that may be the largest Maya city ever discovered!

programme.title: Northwoods Survival
programme.desc: Margot introduces a new "team" of canine friends to her far-flung Yukon homestead; On a remote island in Newfoundland, Matty makes a big move with a home that is far from new; Billy races to replace his ailing roof before the fast-approaching Canadian winter arrives; and Emilie and Gilbert take to the backwoods on a bush-building adventure they hope will keep them safer from the larger, local fauna.

programme.title: Northwoods Survival
programme.desc: In the Yukon, Margot takes her fledgling dog sled team on their inaugural multi-day trapping trip. Billy takes to the woods in search of a more traditional way of living off and on the land. Matty goes all in on the renovation of his ancient Newfoundland seaside home, while Emilie and Gilbert take advantage of an early freeze-up and head out trapping, in search of an essential resource that makes its winter home beneath the ice. Final

programme.title: Ice Rroad Rescue
programme.desc: "Ice Road Rescue" portrays a group of heroic tow truck rescue workers. When winter hits Norway the transport roads turn into treacherous bobsleigh tracks. Every day the rescuers fight to keep this routes open by rescuing heavy trailers stuck in the mountain terrain. Winter storms, avalanches and lack of daylight through the winter season are just some of the challenges they have to face, working close to the polar circle. Extreme winter conditions that often make them risk their lives. "Ice Road Rescue" shows the ultimate man versus nature.

programme.title: Ice Rroad Rescue
programme.desc: "Ice Road Rescue" portrays a group of heroic tow truck rescue workers. When winter hits Norway the transport roads turn into treacherous bobsleigh tracks. programme.title: Construction Fails
programme.desc: When a bridge on an interstate highway collapses, investigators realize the problem might not be the vehicles moving across it, but the ones that aren't. Speaking of things handling heavy traffic, one New York City garage learns why supporting columns are called supporting columns. Also, what went wrong with collapsing Chinese roads and a collapsing French airport, a burning question about apartment block cladding, and why a supertall skyscraper went crooked.

programme.title: Construction Fails
programme.desc: In Thailand, elevated highways keep falling down. A fire breaks out at a bakery in a Uruguayan shopping mall, and a bridge in Colombia collapses just before construction is finished. London's skyline has become a buffet of unsightly skyscrapers, and trucks everywhere are overloaded. Without proper shoring, a building in Iowa crumbles and houses in Rodanthe keep plunging into the ocean.

programme.title: Air Crash Investigation
programme.desc: Every time a plane crashes, the world takes notice. And so do the experts whose job it is to figure out what happened. Air Crash Investigation uncovers the truth behind the most legendary aviation disasters, and every episode features eyewitness accounts, captivating reenactments, state-of-the-art CGI, and interviews with the investigators who ultimately determined what went wrong. And can investigators figure out what caused the crash?

programme.title: Northwoods Survival
programme.desc: Margot takes another crack at backcountry bridge building, but this time she's determined to use the Yukon's harsh conditions to her advantage. Resolved to thrive in the Northwoods, Billy tries his hand at building a blacksmith's forge. Emilie and Gilbert make the most of the frigid temperatures and begin work on a summertime necessity. Locked in the depths of a Newfoundland winter, Matty takes to the sea in search of a new supply of firewood.

programme.title: Northwoods Survival
programme.desc: Locked in the depths of winter, Margot hits the trapping trail - on the hunt for precious fur-bearing resources; Determined to thrive in the frigid Northwoods, Billy tries his hand at building a hot water heating system. Matty makes some serious "comfort" upgrades to his recently expanded Newfoundland abode; And taking a break from cabin construction, Emilie and Gilbert turn their attention to securing a traditional and essential food source.

programme.title: Ultimate Airport Dubai
programme.desc: Dubai International is the global commuter's crossroads. Though the airport and airlines try to prepare for every eventuality, more than one million passengers every week inevitably bring regular and unusual challenges. Throw in the uncontrollable - fog, faulty alarms in the brand new concourse, no show passengers - and it's business as usual for staff who have to think on their feet, fast, to keep the airport running on time, and on track.

programme.title: Ultimate Airport Dubai
programme.desc: Dubai International is in the global race to be the world's biggest airport. A massive new concourse for the world's largest fleet of A380's uses over a 150 million man-hours and 930 thousand cubic meters of concrete. Unafraid of new challenges, the airport was the first ever to close two runways back to back for construction. In the words of its Engineering CEO, "the bar is getting higher and higher." There is no room for failure in Dubai.

programme.title: Black Pharaohs: Sunken Treasures
programme.desc: In a land of gold and ancient treasure, a recent discovery inside a pyramid sends archaeologists in search of clues about the glorious Nubian kingdom. But this isnt an average excavation this pyramid is flooded with water. For the first time ever, archaeologists dive inside underwater pyramid tombs to shed new light on the ancient Nubian kingdom who overthrew the pharaohs and ruled over all of Egypt for 75 years.

With the help of new technology he tracks down the mighty warrior conqueror and reveals his capital - that may be the largest Maya city ever discovered!

programme.title: Science of Stupid
programme.desc: Science of Stupid, series 8. Dallas Campbell hosts the show that combines serious science with some of the craziest, most spectacular user generated clips ever recorded. In each programme we show 40 to 50 clips of misadventure: ordinary folk making extraordinary mistakes and paying the price. We analyse the science behind the mishaps, with the help of bespoke animation and super slow-motion cinematography.

programme.title: Science of Stupid
programme.desc: The show that combines cold hard science with some of the craziest, most spectacular and painful user generated clips ever recorded is back for a third season. Richard Hammond introduces a host of new mishaps featuring brave, if misguided individuals from around the world and then explains the science behind their failure and humiliation with the use of bespoke animations and super slo-mo cinematography. Each week we show between 50 and 60 clips of misadventure - ordinary folk making extraordinary mistakes. Each episode will feature several themes such as motorbike drifting, wakeboarding or attempting to fly drones. Clips are paused, re-wound, and re-played as we analyse exactly what went wrong and why. Richard Hammond explains the physics, chemistry and biology at play, by presenting forensic detail to explain the stupidity that resulted in failure. He'll study everything from acceleration to aero dynamics, fluid pressure to combustion and human anatomy to flammability. This is misadventure explained. This is The Science of Stupid.

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Making motoring dreams come true for a fifth season Car SOS is back, and this time it's bigger and better than ever. Everyone's favourite petrol heads Fuzz Townshend and Tim Shaw continue their mission to seek out classic cars with deserving owners. Using all their guile and restoration knowledge their aim is to sneak away clapped out classics without their owners knowing then in a blizzard of grease and grime and hard graft get the much loved motors back where they deserve to be, on the road. In this series the boys take on their biggest challenges ever. Chronic rust, hard to find parts and broken engines test the boys to their limits. British classics, Italian roadsters, German grand tourers and French hot hatches from across seven decades of motoring history make the most astonishing line-up Car SOS has ever had, and, for their lucky owners the surprise they will never forget.

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Tim and Fuzz are off to the home counties where a 1955 Ford Popular awaits. However, this is no bog-standard classic, it's a highly modified hot-rod with a brute of a V8 engine and a whole host of problems. The car was once the pride and joy of cerebral palsy sufferer Andy, but with so many mechanical troubles his beloved "Pop" has been rotting away in storage with virtually no chance of getting it back on the road. Until, that is, his wife wrote in to Car SOS.

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Tim and Fuzz restore Adam's Datsun 240k Skyline, a rare Japanese classic that hails from the 1970s.After a long spell of ill health Adam's wife is desperate for him to have his beloved car back on the road, but with only two other similar cars currently registered on the road, not only is this the rarest vehicle the Car SOS team has ever taken on, it means part finding is an almost impossible task.

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Tim and Fuzz take on one of the most iconic cars to have ever come out of Germany, a BMW E30 Alpina. The car belongs to car obsessive Maz, a town planner from Walsall, whose dream to restore the car came to an end when he was struck down with a near fatal genetic disorder. Once the car arrives at the workshop not only do the boys discover that it's full of rot, but that it might not actually be the special edition it proports to be. Alpina or not Alpina - that is the question!

programme.title: Tucci in Italy
programme.desc: The region of Le Marche sits on the Adriatic coast of Italy, and though it's lesser known to international tourists, Stanley unearths culinary treasures worthy of recognition. He samples mosciole, plump hand-fished mussels, served in a surprising recipe and discovers three-star Michelin food in a beachside hut. He also tastes vincigrassi, a dish resembling lasagna but regarded as far superior by locals, which originates from one of the earliest cookbooks on record.

programme.title: Tucci in Italy
programme.desc: Stanley immerses himself in the myths and mysteries of this wild island, discovering a cuisine that's as unique as its geography. He travels into the hills to reveal the secrets of longevity, shares a seaside pasta meal with royalty, cooks a pig underground, and learns how a much-loved recipe has its roots in ancient "fairy caves."

programme.title: To Catch a Smuggler: Tropical Takedown
programme.desc: Air and Marine Operations agents in Fajardo chase a vessel to a marina and suspects flee, leaving over 280 kilos of cocaine hidden inside. Mayaguez AMO agents provide armed transport for the USCG, transferring a murder suspect from sea to airfield. Later, they intercept a migrant vessel by shooting out its engine. Agents in the U.S. Virgin Island seize over $750k in cash from a smuggling vessel.

programme.title: To Catch a Smuggler
programme.desc: On the west coast of Puerto Rico, Air and Marine Operations chase down and arrest smugglers caught tossing bales of cocaine overboard. At the Fort Lauderdale Airport, U.S. Customs and Border Protection seizes over a thousand hydrocodone pills from an airline pilot returning from Colombia. At Atlanta International Airport, CBP consults with archaeologists to verify the authenticity of several artifacts they suspect were looted.

programme.title: To Catch a Smuggler
programme.desc: Even during a pandemic, smuggling never stops. After adjusting tactics in the early days of the COVID crisis to meet the increasing flow of narcotics coming across our southern land borders, the Department of Homeland Security is now pivoting back to our nation's airports where international travel is starting to rev back up again. Whether narco-traffickers try to slip through an airport, seaport or land crossing, vigilant officers are on high-alert to stem the flow of illegal contraband. Using every tool available to them, DHS will stop at nothing to catch a smuggler.

programme.title: Airport Security: Peru and Brazil
programme.desc: The intelligence team of the Federal Police has detected two passengers who could be traveling with drugs, but thanks to the work of the officers, they manage to intercept four suspects. Meanwhile, immigration agents must use all their imagination to communicate and gain the trust of a group of passengers who are apparently trying to enter the country irregularly. For their part, the customs agents must prevent counterfeit merchandise from entering the country.

programme.title: Airport Security: Peru
programme.desc: Security teams at Lima, Peru's airport work 24-7 to stop illegal activity. The biggest threat: cocaine smuggling. A US citizen is caught with cocaine - and her story doesn't add up. A woman with cocaine in her suitcase comes clean and a police sting nets another suspect. A Japanese karaoke star is carrying more than music. A carrier implicates her mom. A false positive.

programme.title: Air Crash Investigation
programme.desc: Chalks Ocean Airways Flight #101 Grumman Turbo Mallard (sea plane) December 19th, 2005 Destined for the Bahamas, Chalks Ocean Airways Flight #101 is preparing for takeoff in the waters off Miami Beach, Florida. Part plane, part speedboat, the twin turbo-prop taxis through one of the worlds busiest ports, and after a smooth takeoff, climbs towards the clouds. A minute later, however, just as the plane is climbing through 500 feet, it is suddenly rocked by an explosion and, from the beach, a tourist films the aircrafts catastrophic plunge into the ocean. Lifeguards immediately race to the crash site but there are no survivors. Eyewitness reports and the tourist video confirm one of its wings ripped off in mid-air. But can investigators determine why?

programme.title: Air Crash Investigation
programme.desc: Every time a plane crashes, the world takes notice. And so do the experts whose job it is to figure out what happened. Air Crash Investigation uncovers the truth behind the most legendary aviation disasters, and every episode features eyewitness accounts, captivating reenactments, state-of-the-art CGI, and interviews with the investigators who ultimately determined what went wrong. This season on Air Crash Investigation: Conflicting stories emerge after a Swedish cargo plane spirals to the ground; an African airliner crashes in the wilderness and the cause proves too sinister to be believed; and, when a Pakistani passenger jet disappears on approach to Kathmandu, Nepalese investigators must determine how it ended up on a crash course with the Himalayas.

programme.title: Air Crash Investigation
programme.desc: In California's Mojave Desert, Virgin Galactic test pilots are at the controls of a revolutionary aircraft designed for sub-orbital flight. But as the spaceship accelerates toward the outer reaches of Earth's atmosphere, approaching the speed of sound, it breaks apart and falls from the sky. With the future of commercial space travel hanging in the balance, it's up to investigators to figure out what went wrong.

programme.title: Chernobyl: Inside the Meltdown
programme.desc: At 1.23AM on April 26th,1986 a massive explosion rips through reactor number four at Chernobyl nuclear power plant in the Soviet Ukraine. A safety test has triggered the worst nuclear accident in history, releasing 400 times more radiation into the atmosphere than the Hiroshima bomb. While control room operators reel in shock, the nuclear plant's firefighters battle the blaze amidst lethal levels of radiation. Just 3km away in the local town of Pripyat, Communist party officials encourage life to go on as normal. Children go to school and couples get married while radiation rains down on them. Meanwhile a giant radioactive cloud starts to circle the globe. Episode 1 follows the first 24-hours of the disaster, with first-hand accounts from people desperately trying to make sense of an accident no one thought could ever happen.

programme.title: Chernobyl: Inside the Meltdown
programme.desc: On 28th April, fire still rages in what's left of reactor number 4. Helicopter crews trying to put it out are bombarded by massive doses of radiation. Soviet authorities finally order the evacuation of local residents, and 50,000 people are forced to leave their homes. They are told to take enough for 3 days, but they will never come back. Frontline firefighters suffering acute radiation burns are locked in wards controlled by the KGB. Many will die within days. In Sweden high levels of radiation alert the rest of the world to a radioactive cloud sweeping across Europe - evidence of a massive nuclear accident in the USSR. But the Soviet authorities remain silent. Episode Two charts the first two weeks after the meltdown, revealing the human cost of the accident, and the Soviet efforts to conceal the true scale of the disaster.

programme.title: Chernobyl: Inside the Meltdown
programme.desc: Two weeks after the explosion, Soviet authorities race to stop a molten nuclear core from poisoning the water table, and clear deadly radioactive fallout across a 30km exclusion zone. Through the summer of 1986, hundreds of thousands of conscripts and volunteers answer the government's call, to brave lethal radiation levels and build a giant concrete sarcophagus around the destroyed reactor. Meanwhile in Vienna, after months of speculation, the world's nuclear experts gather to hear the Soviet account of what caused the catastrophe. Is Gorbachev's promise of Glasnost finally revealing the truth from behind the Iron Curtain? Or could this be the Soviet's biggest cover up yet? Episode Three uncovers the extraordinary battle to build the most perilous construction project in history told by those who those who were there 40 years ago.

programme.title: Chernobyl: Inside the Meltdown
programme.desc: Over a year after the accident, plant operators accused of causing the catastrophe face a Soviet show trial. Inside the power plant, nearly 200 tonnes of lethally radioactive nuclear fuel has gone missing. Scientists hunt for it deep inside the perilous reactor core. As the USSR collapses, an independent investigation searches for evidence of what happened the night of the meltdown, uncovering classified documents that reveal what really caused the explosion, and powerful evidence for a Soviet cover up. Chernobyl's toxic legacy continues right up to the present-day war in Ukraine, when the nuclear exclusion zone turns into a war zone, and the power plant itself comes under attack, raising fears of another nuclear disaster. Episode four reveals how 40-years on, the Chernobyl catastrophe is more relevant than ever.

programme.title: Rome Underground
programme.desc: Featuring the parallel stories of a supersize building project and a jaw-dropping archaeological adventure, Rome Underground takes viewers on an immersive journey deep under the Eternal City — revealing how the construction of a 26-kilometer subway line through the heart of historic Rome has literally unearthed some of the most important discoveries of the modern age. At the intersection of technology and archaeology, this documentary chronicles the ambitious mega-project from every angle. With state-of-the-art modelling and 3D animation, it brings the ancient world vividly to life.

programme.title: Hidden Beneath the Cities
programme.desc: In Midyat, Turkey, archaeologists uncover the vast underground city of Matiate, stretching across tunnels and chambers carved thousands of years ago. Evidence of churches, synagogues and Roman coins suggests it was once a refuge for people fleeing religious persecution. In Royston, England, workmen stumble upon a mysterious chalk-carved chamber filled with intricate religious carvings. Beneath Orvieto, Italy, a wine cellar excavation reveals Cavità 254, a striking Etruscan subterranean complex. Once a quarry or water system, its deliberate infilling with pottery and bones hints at ritual or symbolic burial practices. Finally, in Abarkuh, Iran, archaeologists map a massive subterranean tunnel network. Likely part of an ancient qanat water system, exemplifying Persian ingenuity, it used engineering to survive drought, war and desert heat.

programme.title: Ice Road Rescue
programme.desc: "Ice Road Rescue" portrays a group of heroic tow truck rescue workers. When winter hits Norway the transport roads turn into treacherous bobsleigh tracks. Every day the rescuers fight to keep this routes open by rescuing heavy trailers stuck in the mountain terrain. Winter storms, avalanches and lack of daylight through the winter season are just some of the challenges they have to face, working close to the polar circle. Extreme winter conditions that often make them risk their lives. "Ice Road Rescue" shows the ultimate man versus nature.

programme.title: Ice Road Rescue
programme.desc: "Ice Road Rescue" portrays a group of heroic tow truck rescue workers. When winter hits Norway the transport roads turn into treacherous bobsleigh tracks. Every day the rescuers fight to keep this routes open by rescuing heavy trailers stuck in the mountain terrain. A woman with cocaine in her suitcase comes clean and a police sting nets another suspect. A Japanese karaoke star is carrying more than music. A carrier implicates her mom. A false positive.

programme.title: Airport Security: Colombia
programme.desc: El Dorado International Airport officials must put their sagacity and experience to the test in order to detect the boldest and most original attempts at breaking the law that take place at the airport. In this episode, three passengers are caught carrying a total of 120 cocaine capsules inside their bodies and they in turn confess the dramatic circumstances they claim pushed them into committing a crime. Immigration agents face the challenge of deporting eight Bangladeshi nationals who are traveling using false documentation. Also, a young man shows great imagination when he hides a stash of cocaine in an unexpected part of his luggage. And a Dutch passenger dares to carry large quantities of cocaine in the fake bottom of both his suitcases. Meanwhile, customs officials work tirelessly to stop undeclared cash from entering the country, as it is sometimes linked to criminal activity.

programme.title: Airport Security: Peru
programme.desc: Security teams at Peru's biggest airport work 24-7 to stop illegal activity. The biggest problem: cocaine smuggling. Two passengers are carrying cocaine in their bodies. But one has a bigger problem: she says she's pregnant. X-ray scanners flag a suitcase containing only six jackets. But these aren't normal jackets: they've been stitched up with seams of cocaine. In the mail facility, two apparently whole blocks of metal hide a load of cocaine.

programme.title: Air Crash Investigation
programme.desc: En route to the Shetland Islands, a high-tech turboprop is struck by lightning, temporarily blinding the crew and disabling their controls. The pilots declare a Mayday as the plane plunges into a terrifying nosedive. Just seconds before impact, they manage to regain control. And once they're safely on the ground, investigators uncover an unusual feature in the plane that plays a key role in this calamity.

programme.title: Air Crash Investigation
programme.desc: Chalks Ocean Airways Flight #101 Grumman Turbo Mallard (sea plane) December 19th, 2005 Destined for the Bahamas, Chalks Ocean Airways Flight #101 is preparing for takeoff in the waters off Miami Beach, Florida. programme.title: Ice Road Rescue

Every day the rescuers fight to keep this routes open by rescuing heavy trailers stuck in the mountain terrain. Winter storms, avalanches and lack of daylight through the winter season are just some of the challenges they have to face, working close to the polar circle. Extreme winter conditions that often make them risk their lives. "Ice Road Rescue" shows the ultimate man versus nature.

programme.title: Crocodile - Australia's Greatest Survivor
programme.desc: In Australia, dominant saltwater crocodiles, called ""boss crocs," are the largest and most powerful animals on the continent. Even after unrelenting human pressure on their very survival, they are still the masters of their world. Over the last 200 years, the impact of human activities—such as commercial hunting, agriculture and population growth—pushed them to the brink of extinction. Today, these "boss crocs" have continued to not only survive but thrive.

programme.title: Polar Worlds with Bertie Gregory Mashup
programme.desc: National Geographic filmmaker Bertie Gregory travels to the gateways to the poles to film wildlife in two of the harshest environments on the planet: South Georgia Island above Antarctica, and Canada's frigid North below the Arctic Circle. Surviving in these vastly different environments require cunning, persistence and the ability to adapt to a changing climate. This is the story of Bertie's quest to capture these wild environments firsthand.

programme.title: Africa's Wild Geniuses
programme.desc: National Geographic Wild. Africa, with its diverse ecosystems from arid deserts to lush forests, is home to some remarkable wild geniuses. While these outliers may not be the strongest, they compensate with their wit.

programme.title: Valley of the Wolves
programme.desc: Imagine a place that holds everything you've ever wanted. High slopes and good timber for cover and den sites. Water. Your very own herds to hunt and all the wide open space you need for the chase. But others of your kind also want this place. They will challenge you for it. How far will you go to defend it? In the northeast corner of Yellowstone, the wolves of the Druid Peak Pack have long held sway, but their power is waning. The old leaders of the clan cannot stand against the challenge of the invading Slough Creek Pack. But the conquering wolves will find this no safe haven. Here, they will meet a strange and terrible fate. And though in exile, the Druids will rebuild their clan and gather the strength to return. In this beautiful but dangerous place, there is bounty and cruelty, loyalty and banishment, triumph and death. This is the story of the battle for the perfect valley and the high price wolves will pay for paradise.

programme.title: Kingdom of the Apes: Battle Lines
programme.desc: Deep in the jungles of Africa, chimpanzees and gorillas, are living out epic dramas: warriors battle, kings fall, and battle lines are drawn. These unique, character-driven stories will touch your heart and blow your mind.

programme.title: Snow School
programme.desc: When every day is a snow day, school is always in session! Snowy climates can make for harsh living, and adulting is hard no matter where you are, but these young are flourishing; a baby walrus gets a swim lesson from Mom, young stoats' playtime teaches them how to hunt, otter pups take a slide and learn to avoid predators, and owlets practice leaving the nest. We'll also see penguin chicks daring to take the leap, an unlikely team of mismatched teenagers, and others learning how to weather and thrive in the coldest climates, thanks to their snowy homeschooling, some very dedicated parents, and a lot of trial and error.

programme.title: Kingdom of the Apes: Battle Lines
programme.desc: Deep in the jungles of Africa, chimpanzees and gorillas, are living out epic dramas: warriors battle, kings fall, and battle lines are drawn. These unique, character-driven stories will touch your heart and blow your mind.

In this beautiful but dangerous place, there is bounty and cruelty, loyalty and banishment, triumph and death. programme.title: Kingdom of the Apes: Battle Lines
programme.desc: Deep in the jungles of Africa, chimpanzees and gorillas, are living out epic dramas: warriors battle, kings fall, and battle lines are drawn. These unique, character-driven stories will touch your heart and blow your mind.

programme.title: Dr Oakley: Yukon Vet
programme.desc: No two days are ever the same for Dr. Michelle Oakley, the only all-species vet for hundreds of miles in the Yukon. She could be chasing boars by helicopter, stalking wolverines in the wilderness, performing surgery on a jaguar, or facing any number of new challenges with the amazing animals of the north. Animals are incredibly important to the people of the Yukon, and Dr. Oakley will do anything to keep them safe and healthy.

programme.title: Dr Oakley: Yukon Vet
programme.desc: No two days are ever the same for Dr. Michelle Oakley, the only all-species vet for hundreds of miles in the Yukon. She could be chasing boars by helicopter, stalking wolverines in the wilderness, performing surgery on a jaguar, or facing any number of new challenges with the amazing animals of the north. Animals are incredibly important to the people of the Yukon, and Dr. Oakley will do anything to keep them safe and healthy.

programme.title: Winter's Hidden Wonders
programme.desc: Winter pushes wildlife to the extreme. For some, it is a chance to thrive; for others, the only way to escape the icy hold of winter is to shut down every physical system. When spring finally comes, it is a time for celebration for those who managed to survive the most brutal of seasons.

programme.title: Africa's Deadly Kingdom
programme.desc: Instead of providing in-depth examinations of the fauna of the continent of Africa, this nature series alternatively focuses on the hostile and unforgiving landscapes that the animals have to survive in. Africa is home to some of the most extreme and diverse environments on the planet, from arid deserts to lush rainforests, mountainous peaks to mighty river and lake systems, savannah grasslands to rugged coastline. This show explores these different regions and documents how the plants and creatures that inhabit them endure places where there are few resources of food and water or where competition is rife and life-threatening. The unique demands placed on these plants and animals have resulted in the development of incredibly resilient species that remain successful against all the odds.

programme.title: Austria's Wild Heritage - One Country Six National Parks
programme.desc: The first part explains the beginnings of the national parks in Austria, which were characterized by the visions of a few pioneers and the civil disobedience of many

programme.title: Winter's Hidden Wonders
programme.desc: Winter pushes wildlife to the extreme. For some, it is a chance to thrive; for others, the only way to escape the icy hold of winter is to shut down every physical system. When spring finally comes, it is a time for celebration for those who managed to survive the most brutal of seasons.

The unique demands placed on these plants and animals have resulted in the development of incredibly resilient species that remain successful against all the odds.

programme.title: Africa's Deadly Kingdom
programme.desc: Africa's southern coastline hosts some of the most vibrant, varied and dynamic reef systems on the planet. Bizarre, beautiful and mysterious worlds, they are crammed with a bustling diversity of life. Ever changing, these coral gardens are amongst the most competitive and cutthroat of underwater environments. Africa's Deadly Kingdom: Reefs dives in to reveal the astounding adaptations of a multitude of marine creatures to life below the waves.

programme.title: Africa's Deadly Kingdom
programme.desc: Instead of providing in-depth examinations of the fauna of the continent of Africa, this nature series alternatively focuses on the hostile and unforgiving landscapes that the animals have to survive in. Africa is home to some of the most extreme and diverse environments on the planet, from arid deserts to lush rainforests, mountainous peaks to mighty river and lake systems, savannah grasslands to rugged coastline. The unique demands placed on these plants and animals have resulted in the development of incredibly resilient species that remain successful against all the odds.

programme.title: Austria's Wild Heritage - One Country Six National Parks
programme.desc: The first part explains the beginnings of the national parks in Austria, which were characterized by the visions of a few pioneers and the civil disobedience of many

programme.title: Austria's Wild Heritage - One Country Six National Parks
programme.desc: The second part focuses on the challenges of the future: How do the national parks deal with invasive species from outside and what success stories can be reported? For instance, in the Hohe Tauern National Park the previously extinct ibexes and bearded vultures have been reintroduced. In the Gesäuse National Park Avalanches created one of the most species-rich habitats, which rare butterflies like the Red Apollo colonize.

programme.title: Winter's Hidden Wonders
programme.desc: Winter pushes wildlife to the extreme. For some, it is a chance to thrive; for others, the only way to escape the icy hold of winter is to shut down every physical system. When spring finally comes, it is a time for celebration for those who managed to survive the most brutal of seasons.

When spring finally comes, it is a time for celebration for those who managed to survive the most brutal of seasons.

programme.desc: Winter pushes wildlife to the extreme. For some, it is a chance to thrive; for others, the only way to escape the icy hold of winter is to shut down every physical system. When spring finally comes, it is a time for celebration for those who managed to survive the most brutal of seasons.

programme.title: The Incredible Dr. Pol
programme.desc: With more than 19,000 patients, Dr. Pol has seen it all. Specializing in large farm animals, this senior is anything but retiring as he takes an old-school, non-nonsense approach to veterinary medicine. Unflappable and unstoppable, this doc routinely puts in 14-hour days and is a legend in his community. This new series travels with him across rural Michigan to care for every family pet and head of livestock in need of his expertise and kindness.

programme.title: The Incredible Dr. Pol

programme.title: Killer Snakes
programme.desc: Packed with a potent neurotoxic venom, one family of snakes kill more people than any other on earth. Scientists around the world race to uncover how.

programme.title: Desert Lions: Surviving At Skeleton Coast
programme.desc: In this true story, lion researcher Dr. Philip Stander follows three orphaned lion cubs from birth to adulthood as they navigate the complexity of survival in one of the world's most unforgiving environments, Namibia's deadly Skeleton Coast. He gets up close and personal to unlock secrets of their world and witness the amazing adaptability of these special cats.

Bizarre, beautiful and mysterious worlds, they are crammed with a bustling diversity of life. Ever changing, these coral gardens are amongst the most competitive and cutthroat of underwater environments. Africa's Deadly Kingdom: Reefs dives in to reveal the astounding adaptations of a multitude of marine creatures to life below the waves.

programme.title: Africa's Deadly Kingdom
programme.desc: Instead of providing in-depth examinations of the fauna of the continent of Africa, this nature series alternatively focuses on the hostile and unforgiving landscapes that the animals have to survive in. Africa is home to some of the most extreme and diverse environments on the planet, from arid deserts to lush rainforests, mountainous peaks to mighty river and lake systems, savannah grasslands to rugged coastline. This show explores these different regions and documents how the plants and creatures that inhabit them endure places where there are few resources of food and water or where competition is rife and life-threatening. The unique demands placed on these plants and animals have resulted in the development of incredibly resilient species that remain successful against all the odds.

programme.title: Winter's Hidden Wonders
programme.desc: Winter pushes wildlife to the extreme. For some, it is a chance to thrive; for others, the only way to escape the icy hold of winter is to shut down every physical system. When spring finally comes, it is a time for celebration for those who managed to survive the most brutal of seasons.

programme.title: Africa's Deadly Kingdom
programme.desc: Instead of providing in-depth examinations of the fauna of the continent of Africa, this nature series alternatively focuses on the hostile and unforgiving landscapes that the animals have to survive in. Africa is home to some of the most extreme and diverse environments on the planet, from arid deserts to lush rainforests, mountainous peaks to mighty river and lake systems, savannah grasslands to rugged coastline. This show explores these different regions and documents how the plants and creatures that inhabit them endure places where there are few resources of food and water or where competition is rife and life-threatening. The unique demands placed on these plants and animals have resulted in the development of incredibly resilient species that remain successful against all the odds.

programme.title: Africa's Deadly Kingdom
programme.desc: Africa's southern coastline hosts some of the most vibrant, varied and dynamic reef systems on the planet. Bizarre, beautiful and mysterious worlds, they are crammed with a bustling diversity of life. Ever changing, these coral gardens are amongst the most competitive and cutthroat of underwater environments. Africa's Deadly Kingdom: Reefs dives in to reveal the astounding adaptations of a multitude of marine creatures to life below the waves.

programme.title: Dr Oakley: Yukon Vet
programme.desc: No two days are ever the same for Dr. Michelle Oakley, the only all-species vet for hundreds of miles in the Yukon. She could be chasing boars by helicopter, stalking wolverines in the wilderness, performing surgery on a jaguar, or facing any number of new challenges with the amazing animals of the north. Animals are incredibly important to the people of the Yukon, and Dr. Oakley will do anything to keep them safe and healthy.

programme.title: Dr Oakley: Yukon Vet
programme.desc: No two days are ever the same for Dr. Michelle Oakley, the only all-species vet for hundreds of miles in the Yukon. She could be chasing boars by helicopter, stalking wolverines in the wilderness, performing surgery on a jaguar, or facing any number of new challenges with the amazing animals of the north. Animals are incredibly important to the people of the Yukon, and Dr. Oakley will do anything to keep them safe and healthy.

programme.title: World's Biggest Bullshark
programme.desc: Ecologist, Dr. Neil Hammerschlag teams up with former commando, James Glancy and Evolutionary Biologist, Dr. Toby Daly-Engel to uncover how Big Bull grew to become a giant and find out if there could be more like her.

programme.title: Planet Carnivore: Perfect Killers
programme.desc: Great white sharks, polar bears, lions and brown bears battle rivals and the elements to maintain their positions at the top of the food chain.

programme.title: Critter Fixers: Country Vets: Compilations
programme.desc: The Critter Fixers prove time and again they are the best vets for just about any job: When a dog attacks a cat and a woman, the docs treat not only the cat but also its owner. A Nigerian goat needs the docs' help with a UTI; a police dog has ticks and cysts that need attention; a baby rhea bird has a leg injury; and Johnny the mule needs help with some painful hooves.

programme.title: World's Biggest Bullshark
programme.desc: Ecologist, Dr. Neil Hammerschlag teams up with former commando, James Glancy and Evolutionary Biologist, Dr. Toby Daly-Engel to uncover how Big Bull grew to become a giant and find out if there could be more like her.

programme.title: Planet Carnivore: Perfect Killers
programme.desc: Great white sharks, polar bears, lions and brown bears battle rivals and the elements to maintain their positions at the top of the food chain.

programme.title: World's Deadliest Whale
programme.desc: Intelligent, cultured and deadly, the killer whale dominates every ocean on earth and as our planet changes, this top predator changes with it. They are elite, marine hunters with an extremely diverse diet including other apex predators like the great white shark. Killer whales pursue their prey like a pack of wolves and have multiple killing techniques.

programme.title: The Incredible Dr. Pol
programme.desc: With more than 19,000 patients, Dr. Pol has seen it all. Specializing in large farm animals, this senior is anything but retiring as he takes an old-school, non-nonsense approach to veterinary medicine. Unflappable and unstoppable, this doc routinely puts in 14-hour days and is a legend in his community. This new series travels with him across rural Michigan to care for every family pet and head of livestock in need of his expertise and kindness.

programme.title: The Incredible Dr. Pol
programme.desc: With more than 19,000 patients, Dr. Pol has seen it all. programme.title: Sharks of the North
programme.desc: Sightings of great white sharks have been on the rise in Atlantic Canada. Alanna Canaran, a passionate science educator and dive instructor, embarks on a mission to unravel the enigmas surrounding these magnificent creatures and is determined to dispel fear of sharks in Nova Scotians. As the journey unfolds, Canaran and her team dive into the history of human-shark relationships along the coast. Facing numerous challenges, including elusive sharks and treacherous weather conditions, the team perseveres. With sheer determination and a stroke of luck, they gather invaluable knowledge of these magnificent creatures, contributing to a better understanding of white sharks in Canada.

programme.title: Return of the White Shark
programme.desc: Cape Cod — America's Playground — is a newly emerging hotspot for a top predator. Great Whites are appearing in increasing numbers, when for centuries there were none. Through spectacular imagery, we'll see how the landscape of Cape Cod has changed over time, and what is luring Great Whites in record numbers.

programme.title: World's Biggest Bullshark
programme.desc: Ecologist, Dr. Neil Hammerschlag teams up with former commando, James Glancy and Evolutionary Biologist, Dr. Toby Daly-Engel to uncover how Big Bull grew to become a giant and find out if there could be more like her.

programme.title: Cannibal Sharks
programme.desc: National Geographic Wild. Cannibal Sharks pulls in two of the world's leading shark experts to unpack and analyze the mounting evidence that shark-on-shark attacks happen a lot more than we ever realized.

programme.title: Perfect Predator
programme.desc: From their massive size, strength and stamina to their finely tuned hunting skills and unrelenting will to survive—sharks have evolved to be the perfect predator. But how do they do it? In this action-packed hour, unlock key evolutionary components that have kept these apex predators on top for millions of years.

programme.title: The Incredible Dr. Pol

This new series travels with him across rural Michigan to care for every family pet and head of livestock in need of his expertise and kindness.

programme.title: Untamed Philippines
programme.desc: We start on the tiny island of Batan, meeting a unique viper and a ubiquitous but also giant, land crab. Luzon is the main island of the Philippines. We drop in to Mindoro to see the most endangered large mammal in the Philippines, the tamaraw.

programme.title: Untamed Philippines
programme.desc: Moving from turtles of the Palawan coast to the unique National Marine Park at Tubbataha, to meet giants, whale sharks and manta rays. We also see how people can mediate between people and wildlife as we join a snake hunting team catching and translocating a deadly Samar cobra.
